phase-summary.outcome: Internal quote workflow, dashboard UX, scorecard, liabilities, and asset inventory plus growth guidance are documented and surfaced in the admin portal. `/ops` shows KPI cards above the command center, and the command center now uses one left rail that combines section switching with inline detail content while all live tab content stays in the right column. Admins get sidebar links to both `/ops` and customer `/dashboard` preview; optional `PORTAL_ADMIN_ONLY=true` restricts portal routes to admin users. Liabilities and scorecard rows are Payload collections (`ops-liability-items`, `ops-scorecard-rows`, migration 20260325_005955_ops_liabilities_and_scorecard_rows) merged with code defaults, seeded into real records, and used by the live dashboard; manual scorecard values now drive KPI cards where applicable. The day board focuses on Payload service appointments, while projected revenue and MRR now read from first-party quote and service-plan records when available instead of third-party CRM reads. Ops assets are managed as a real inventory in the workspace-backed `ops-asset-ladder-items` collection, seeded from the current business baseline and editable in-place from Ops workspace rather than treated only as a static ladder. Internal ops targets global adds KPI tooltips, scorecard tooltip rows, optional chart note, and editable quote projection weights (migration `20260327_120000_internal_ops_quote_weights`). When there is no open pipeline or active plans, KPI cards show `$0` with target labels from Internal ops targets (not placeholder dollar amounts). The business momentum chart uses Payload-derived monthly series: paid invoices by `paidAt`, weighted quotes by `updatedAt`, current MRR run rate, and Gross margin manual % when set; falls back to the legacy illustrative series only when no chart data is passed. The public instant quote engine reads draftable/publishable `quoteSettings`; the portal links admins to Quotes and Quote settings. Portal and marketing Joyride tours are implemented end-to-end (`06-10`, `06-11`); believable demo QA for tour copy remains gated by `03-09` / BLOCKERS `BLK-demo-seed-001` (resolved); AI assistant tour deferred to phase `08` / BLOCKERS `BLK-tour-ai-001`.

file.description: Customer dashboard route; admins see same view with optional preview banner (no forced redirect to `/ops`).
Internal admin ops dashboard route with first-party KPI and schedule context.
Portal sidebar shortcuts for admin quote tools and docs.
Left-rail command-center navigator with inline detail panel for daily ops, scorecard, milestones, and assets.
Calendar-based day board with scheduled jobs and service workload context.
Admin-gated Payload service-appointment feed for the ops calendar.
Responsive chart controls that no longer overlap dashboard content.
Admin-facing config for growth and system context.
Dashboard-specific operator board and trend data.
Draftable quote-settings global for instant quote math and service toggles.
Server mapper for published and draft quote settings.
Portal Joyride registry: staff + customer tour IDs, `/ops` tab routing, per-surface steps (TASK-REGISTRY `06-10`).
Marketing-site tour registry (`public-instant-quote`); used by `SiteTourProvider` on `/`.
Portal-wide Joyride provider: context, `?tour=` URL start, high z-index options.
Homepage Joyride for instant quote; `?tour=` sync; no autolaunch except via explicit control.

next.action: Use the completed quote and ops dashboard foundations as stable input for phase 07 CRM, billing, automation, and staff workflow work rather than reopening quote math or KPI presentation patterns.
Backfill real revenue, quote pipeline, and MRR series where seeded/demo data is still standing in for production history.
When phase `08` assistant UI exists, add optional AI beta Joyride (BLOCKERS `BLK-tour-ai-001`); keep tours separate from chat per D-tour-001.
